# Enforcement and Penalties

## Regulatory Bodies

The **Office of the Australian Information Commissioner** (OAIC) is the primary regulator. The ACCC also plays a role in consumer data protection under the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth).

## Penalty Framework

The Privacy Legislation Amendment (Enforcement and Other Measures) Act 2022 (Cth) significantly increased penalties:

| Violation Type | Maximum Penalty (Corporation) |
|---------------|------------------------------|
| Serious interference | $50 million or 30% of turnover |
| Repeated interference | $2.5 million per contravention |
| Failure to notify | $2.5 million |

## Notable Enforcement Actions

- **Australian Information Commissioner v Facebook Inc** [2020] — $20 million penalty
- **Secretary, Department of Health v Medibank** [2023] — ongoing proceedings
- **Privacy Commissioner v Clearview AI** [2021] — ordered to cease collection

> The 2022 amendments brought Australian privacy penalties in line with international standards, signalling Parliament's intent to treat data protection as a serious regulatory matter comparable to competition law.
